source_jobs.title: Business Process Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Lumilens

At Lumilens we are building the critical photonics infrastructure that powers tomorrow’s AI supercomputing. From chip-to-chip optical interconnects to scalable photonic engines, Lumilens is unlocking a new era of computing - faster, cooler, and massively more efficient.

We’re a well-funded startup backed by Mayfield and led by veterans who’ve built and scaled some of the most transformative technologies in the industry. At Lumilens, we’re developing high-speed photonics products purpose-built to power the future of AI infrastructure and high-performance computing.

This isn’t incremental innovation, it’s a ground-floor opportunity to rethink the optical layer from the silicon up. You’ll work alongside a team of world-class engineers solving some of the hardest challenges in scaling optical systems. If you're looking for mission, momentum, and the chance to make an outsized impact, jump on the rocket ship. We’re just getting started.

Position Overview



The Business Management Office (BMO) within the Operations group serves as the central engine for cross-functional process governance, capability building, and operational transformation across the company’s full product lifecycle. This role sits at the intersection of strategy and execution, partnering with Product Sales, Product Design and Engineering, Quality, New Product Introduction (NPI), Supply Chain, Manufacturing Operations, and Customer Service to ensure that the processes enabling these functions are designed for scale, resilience, and competitive differentiation.



The Senior Business Process Manager will take end-to-end responsibility for identifying, designing, automating, and continuously improving business processes across one or more operational domains. This is a high-visibility, high-impact role suited for an experienced practitioner who combines deep process design expertise with hands-on experience in enterprise automation platforms, analytical rigor, and the credibility to influence senior stakeholders without direct authority.



Key Responsibilities

 - Process Design and Architecture

 - Lead the design, documentation, and governance of business process maps across core operational domains including NPI gate reviews, order-to-cash, supply and demand planning, manufacturing execution, and post-sale support workflows.

 - Conduct structured process discovery sessions using interviews, observation, and data analysis to establish current-state baselines and identify root causes of waste, cycle time delay, and quality degradation.

 - Develop future-state process architectures that align with enterprise operating model goals, technology roadmaps, and scalability requirements for a hardware-software hybrid product portfolio.

 - Produce and maintain process artifacts including SIPOC diagrams, swim-lane maps, RACI matrices, control plans, and standard operating procedures (SOPs) at the appropriate level of fidelity for each audience.

 - Ensure process designs incorporate regulatory compliance, export control, trade compliance, and internal audit requirements as applicable to hardware and software product lines.



 - Business Process Automation

 - Define automation opportunity cases across target processes, including RPA candidates, workflow orchestration needs, API integration points, and AI-assisted decision support applications.

 - Partner with Enterprise Architecture, IT, and platform owners to translate process requirements into functional and non-functional specifications for automation tooling such as ServiceNow, Salesforce, SAP, Appian, UiPath, or equivalent platforms.

 - Serve as the business-side process owner and subject matter expert throughout the design, build, test, and deploy lifecycle for automation initiatives, ensuring solutions accurately reflect the intended process logic.

 - Establish monitoring frameworks and KPI dashboards to measure automation performance, exception rates, and business value realization post-dep

source_jobs.title: Senior Compliance Engineer
source_jobs.description:
JOB TITLE: SENIOR COMPLIANCE ENGINEER


ABOUT LUMILENS

At Lumilens, we are building the critical photonics infrastructure powering tomorrow’s AI supercomputing. We develop chip-to-chip optical interconnects and scalable photonic engines, unlocking a new era of faster, cooler, and more efficient computing. Join our rapidly growing team and help shape the infrastructure of the future.

 


POSITION OVERVIEW

We are seeking a Senior Compliance Engineer with 8 to 10 years of relevant experience to ensure our optical transceiver products meet all necessary compliance and certification requirements. In this role, you will be responsible for coordinating and executing end-to-end certification activities for products, including compliance with EMC, safety, RoHS, WEEE, GR468, and relevant international standards. This is a dynamic role requiring collaboration with both local and international labs, adaptability to work in shifts, and the ability to work both independently and as part of a team.

 


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Take ownership for all compliance needs of optical transceiver products, including certification to EMC, safety, RoHS, WEEE, GR468, and other global standards.

 - Maintain thorough knowledge and application of EMC and safety standards for IT devices and equipment.

 - Build and manage a comprehensive certification ecosystem, working with local and international labs to ensure end-to-end compliance.

 - Coordinate lab scheduling and demonstrate flexibility to work in shifts, based on lab availability.

 - Prepare and manage documentation required to support product certifications, ensuring full compliance with product release milestones.

 - Work independently on assigned compliance projects while collaborating closely with cross-functional teams including engineering, product management, and external labs.

 - Track regulatory changes and proactively update compliance processes and product certifications as needed.

 


REQUIRED SKILLS & QUALIFICATIONS

 - 8 to 10 years of experience in handling product compliance for optical transceivers or related electronic hardware, including EMC, safety, RoHS, WEEE, and GR468 standards.

 - Strong knowledge of EMC and safety standards for IT devices/equipment.

 - Proven ability to create and manage relationships with both local and global test labs to support certification efforts.

 - Comfortable with working shifts, as required by lab scheduling.

 - Capable of working independently and collaboratively in cross-functional teams.

 - Excellent organizational and communication skills.

 


WHY JOIN LUMILENS?

 - Competitive salary commensurate with experience

 - Comprehensive benefits including health insurance

 - Professional development and certification support

 - Access to advanced technology and a collaborative, innovative environment

 

Lumilens is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, gender, identity, orientation, veteran status, disability, or any other legally protected status.

source_jobs.title: Verification Lead (Lead DV)
source_jobs.description:
About Lumilens

At Lumilens we are building the critical photonics infrastructure that powers tomorrow’s AI supercomputing. From chip-to-chip optical interconnects to scalable photonic engines, Lumilens is unlocking a new era of computing faster, cooler, and massively more efficient.

We’re a well-funded startup backed by Mayfield and led by veterans who’ve built and scaled some of the most transformative technologies in the industry.

This isn’t incremental innovation, it’s a ground-floor opportunity to rethink the optical layer from the silicon up. The market is moving fast, and we’re moving faster. You’ll work alongside a team of world-class engineers solving some of the hardest challenges in optics, systems, and scale. Every line of code, every design decision, every breakthrough you help deliver will shape the infrastructure of tomorrow.

If you're looking for mission, momentum, and the chance to make an outsized impact, jump on the rocket ship. We’re just getting started.

About the Role

You own verification end to end for a first-of-its-kind chiplet. As a founding hire you define the methodology before there's RTL to verify — this role is on the critical path, and getting the coverage strategy and regression infrastructure right early is the single biggest schedule lever we have.



What You'll Own

 - The full DV strategy: verification plan, coverage model (functional + code), UVM architecture, and signoff criteria from block to full-chip.

 - Stand up regression/CI infrastructure and nightly runs before design hands over first RTL.

 - Verification of the in-house engines against an algorithmic golden model, with programmable error injection and error-pattern coverage.

 - Hardware-assisted verification: bring up and own the emulation / FPGA-prototyping methodology.

 - UCIe VIP and compliance, link-training, loopback, lane-repair scenarios, bit-exact transparency and more.

 - Performance and throughput verification.

 - UVM register verification, firmware co-simulation, and boot flows on the MCU subsystem.

 - Mixed-signal co-simulation — verifying the digital front end against behavioral models of analog/mixed-signal blocks.

 - Gate-level and low-power (UPF) verification as part of signoff.

 - Lead and grow the DV team (across FEC, UCIe, MCU, datapath, and full-chip).

Required

 - 10+ years ASIC/SoC verification, with team leadership on at least one complex SoC from plan to tapeout.

 - Proven leadership verifying large, complex digital SoCs / subsystems, including interconnect fabric and full-chip closure.

 - Deep UVM/SystemVerilog; coverage-driven verification; constrained-random methodology.

 - Hands-on with hardware-assisted verification — emulation (e.g., Palladium/Veloce) and/or FPGA prototyping (e.g., HAPS/Protium).

 - Comfortable with mixed-signal verification (Verilog-AMS or SV real models).

 - Regression/CI at scale (grid, triage automation), and rigorous coverage closure.

Preferred

 - High-speed SerDes, PAM4, or FEC/coding (Reed-Solomon) verification experience.

 - UCIe, PCIe/CXL, Ethernet, UALink or comparable die-to-die / link-layer VIP and compliance experience.

 - NIC Cards, Ethernet/IB Switches, Cache-coherency verification (multi-core / coherent fabric).

 - Low-power/UPF and gate-level simulation methodology.

 - Performance-verification methodology.

 - Formal verification for control/interlock logic; portable-stimulus.

What We Offer

 - Competitive salary commensurate with experience

 - Comprehensive benefits package including health, dental, and vision

 - Professional development opportunities and certification support

 - Access to cutting-edge technology and cloud platforms

 - Collaborative work environment with cross-functional teams

Lumilens is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, gender identity, sexual orientation, veteran status, disability, or any other legally protected status.

We are seeking a Product Quality & Test Reliability Engineer to join our rapidly growing team. This isn't just another role. source_jobs.title: Senior/Staff Process Engineer (Flip Chip)
source_jobs.description:
Core Responsibilities

 1. Owns the process development and technical support of the Flip Chip process for silicon photonic chip wafers, with OSAT or internal line. Process may include plasma cleaning, flux engineering, pick & place, reflow, underfill, curing and/or TCB/NCP flux-less flow.

 2. DOE to characterize process window and margin. Optimize process integration with upstream processes (e.g. bump/FO) to increase yield and manufacturability.

 3. Material Characterization & Selection: Evaluate and qualify new manufacturing raw materials, including NCP, flux, underfill etc, as applicable.

 4. Design and execute DOE plans to characterize thermal and mechanical stress. Lead reliability validations including Temperature Cycling Test (TCT), Unbiased Highly Accelerated Stress Test (HAST) and High-Temperature Storage (HTS).

 5. Failure Analysis & Debug: Own the root-cause diagnostics of structural failure modes unique to FC (e.g. flux void, residue, misalignment, die warpage, reflow anomaly, underfill void etc), and formulate improvement solutions.

 6. Manage incoming material quality control, establish SPC control plans and monitor process variation to ensure long-term process stability.

 7. Compile process SOP, WI, process failure reports and engineering change documents; support NPI new product introduction and process verification.

 

Key Requirements & Qualifications

 - Education: Bachelor or Master degree in Materials Science, Chemical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Microelectronics, or an equivalent technical discipline.

 - Experience: 5+ years of hands-on industry experience in flip chip assembly.

 - Technical Knowledge:

 - Deep practical understanding of plasma cleaning, flux engineering, pick & place, reflow, underfill, curing and/or TCB/NCP flux-less flow.

 - Comprehensive command of finite element simulation principles regarding warpage, thermal dissipation, and structural mechanical integrity.

 - Solid working expertise with industry validation methodologies including FMEA, SPC controls, and structured 8D problem-solving tools.

 - Software Tools: High proficiency with robust analytical platforms like JMP, Minitab.

The Role

This is a hands-on Finance Analyst role based in Singapore, supporting the Singapore FP&A and Controlling teams. The role is focused on operational reporting, data analysis, and process automation across APAC, with strong exposure to budgeting, forecasting, and business partnering in a fast-paced environment.

Key Responsibilities

 - Prepare monthly management reports, variance analysis, and operational dashboards for Singapore and APAC stakeholders.

 - Support budgeting and forecasting cycles across APAC, including data consolidation, trend analysis, and planning support.

 - Build and maintain financial models for supply chain, manufacturing cost, and APAC commercial analysis.

 - Run ad-hoc analytics for business partners, including pricing analysis, customer profitability, and opex deep dives.

 - Partner with data and systems teams to improve reporting automation and streamline finance processes.

 - Support the Singapore month-end close cycle alongside the accounting team, including reporting and analysis deliverables.

Required Qualifications

 - 3–6 years of experience in FP&A, audit, investment banking, or strategy consulting.

 - Strong financial modeling skills and advanced Excel capabilities.

 - Bachelor’s degree in finance, accounting, economics, engineering, or a related field.

 - Strong analytical mindset, attention to detail, and ability to synthesize data into actionable insights.

 - Comfort working in a fast-paced, evolving environment with cross-functional stakeholders.

Preferred Qualifications

 - CPA, CFA, or MBA.

 - Experience in hardware, manufacturing, semiconductor, or technology industries.

 - Exposure to cost accounting, supply chain finance, or commercial finance analysis.

What We Offer

 - A highly visible analyst role with exposure to financial planning, operational reporting, and business decision support across APAC.

 - The opportunity to work at the intersection of finance, operations, and advanced photonics technology in a fast-scaling deep-tech environment.

 - Competitive compensation commensurate with experience, along with comprehensive health benefits.

 - Meaningful exposure to cross-functional and global teams across Singapore and the broader APAC region.

 - Professional growth and development opportunities, including the chance to strengthen systems, improve reporting, and build scalable finance processes.

 - A collaborative, ambitious environment where strong analytical work can make an immediate and measurable impact.



*Lumilens is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, gender, identity, orientation, veteran status, disability, or any other legally protected status.

source_jobs.title: Power Integrity Engineer-Silicon
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT LUMILENS

At Lumilens we are building the critical photonics infrastructure that powers tomorrow’s AI supercomputing. From chip-to-chip optical interconnects to scalable photonic engines, Lumilens is unlocking a new era of computing - faster, cooler, and massively more efficient.

 

We’re a well-funded startup backed by Mayfield and led by veterans who’ve built and scaled some of the most transformative technologies in the industry. At Lumilens, we’re developing high-speed photonics products purpose-built to power the future of AI infrastructure and high-performance computing.

 

This isn’t incremental innovation, it’s a ground-floor opportunity to rethink the optical layer from the silicon up. You’ll work alongside a team of world-class engineers solving some of the hardest challenges in scaling optical systems. Every line of code, every design decision, every breakthrough you help deliver will shape the infrastructure of tomorrow.

 

If you're looking for mission, momentum, and the chance to make an outsized impact, jump on the rocket ship. We’re just getting started.

 

About the Role

Lumilens is revolutionizing AI compute infrastructure with next-generation photonic interconnects, enabling massive scale-out and scale-up architectures through co-packaged optics (CPO), near-packaged optics (NPO), high-density silicon photonics, and advanced heterogeneous integration.

We're looking for a world-class Senior / Principal Power Integrity Engineer to own and drive power delivery strategies across complex 2.5D/3D packages that integrate electrical ICs, photonic ICs, chiplets, and high-bandwidth memory. You'll architect robust, low-noise, high-efficiency PDNs that support extreme power densities, tight voltage margins, and ultra-high-speed signaling—critical for powering the world's largest AI clusters without compromising performance or reliability.

 

Key Responsibilities

 - Define and implement power integrity architectures for 2.5D/3D heterogeneous integration, including chiplet-based designs, interposer/substrate scaling, on-package voltage regulation, multi-rail PDN topologies, decoupling strategies, and power-aware thermal co-design.

 - Perform comprehensive full-stack power integrity analysis and optimization—from die-level IR drop and electromigration to package-level PDN impedance profiling, transient response, and system-level power delivery network (PDN) validation.

 - Model, simulate, and validate power delivery solutions using industry-leading tools (e.g., Ansys SIwave, HFSS, Cadence Sigrity, Apache RedHawk, Voltus, or equivalent) across silicon, interposer, substrate, and board interfaces.

 - Lead power integrity co-design with silicon design, package engineering, and photonics teams to optimize bump maps, power/ground grid layouts, TSV/RDL routing, on-die decoupling, and voltage regulator placement for minimal noise, IR drop, and EMI in CPO/NPO systems.

 - Drive selection and qualification of advanced power components (VRMs, capacitors, inductors, low-loss substrates, advanced TIMs) and collaborate with foundry/OSAT partners on PDN process capabilities, manufacturability, yield, and cost.

 - Develop and refine power integrity methodologies, design rules, and sign-off criteria tailored to high-performance AI accelerators, photonic transceivers, and chiplet architectures.

 - Analyze and mitigate power-related reliability risks, including electromigration, thermal-induced voltage droop, power sequencing, and long-term stability under dynamic AI workloads.

 - Partner with cross-functional teams (architecture, Si design, package design, system engineering, supply chain) to explore emerging technologies, influence roadmaps, generate proof-of-concepts, and protect IP that maximizes power efficiency and performance.

Thermal Engineer

 

About Lumilens

 

At Lumilens, we are building the critical photonics infrastructure that powers tomorrow’s AI supercomputing. From chip-to-chip optical interconnects to scalable photonic engines, Lumilens is unlocking a new era of computing—faster, cooler, and massively more efficient. Join our collaborative team to help shape the future of high-speed optical connectivity.

 

Position Overview

 

As a Thermal Engineer at Lumilens, you will play a key support role in developing and optimizing thermal management solutions for advanced optical interconnect products used in data center and AI infrastructure equipment, including pluggable optics, near-packaged optics, and co-packaged optics. You will work closely with senior engineers to contribute to the design, analysis, and validation of thermal solutions for high-density optoelectronic systems, ensuring product reliability and performance.

 

Responsibilities

 

 - Assist in the thermal design of pluggable optics, near-packaged optics, and co-packaged optics for data center switching, compute, and interconnect equipment.

 - Support the development of air-cooled and liquid-cooled thermal solutions for high-power optical and optoelectronic assemblies, such as heatsinks, thermal bridges, cold plates, coolant interfaces, and platform-level heat rejection paths.

 - Contribute to thermal analysis of interactions among ASICs, DSPs, optical engines, lasers, packages, and surrounding mechanical structures in dense data center deployments.

 - Collaborate with system architects and senior engineers to help define thermal requirements for data center equipment at rack-level, chassis-level, and module-level, under both air and liquid cooling conditions.

 - Apply working knowledge of liquid-cooling technologies used in data center systems, including cold plates, manifolded liquid loops, coolant distribution concepts, and thermal interfaces.

 - Assist in evaluating tradeoffs between air cooling and liquid cooling for power-dense electronics and optoelectronic assemblies.

 - Gain familiarity with the thermal challenges unique to near-packaged and co-packaged optics, such as proximity to hot ASICs, optical temperature sensitivity, thermal isolation, and serviceability constraints.

 

Why Join Lumilens?

 

 - Work at the forefront of next-generation AI and data center technology

 - Collaborative and innovative environment

 - Opportunities for rapid career growth

 - Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits

 

Lumilens is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, or any other protected characteristic.

Every line of code, every design decision, every breakthrough you help deliver will shape the infrastructure of tomorrow.

If you're looking for mission, momentum, and the chance to make an outsized impact, jump on the rocket ship. We’re just getting started.

About the role. You will build the executable model of the chip before silicon exists. Your architecture models will settle the decisions that are expensive to change late, including datapath latency, throughput, buffer sizing, and control-loop tuning. Your reference models will become the golden checkers the verification team scores against, and the platform will run real firmware months ahead of RTL. This is a founding, critical-path role: the virtual platform is what unblocks the firmware and architecture work in parallel with design.

What You'll Own

 - A SystemC/TLM-2.0 virtual platform — MCU (ISS-backed) + peripherals + memory map + a UCIe transactor + host stub — that firmware runs on pre-RTL.

 - Architecture/performance models: architectural trade studies, end-to-end datapath throughput and latency, FIFO/buffer sizing, design-space/configuration exploration.

 - Golden reference models handed to DV as scoreboards.

 - Behavioral models of the analog and control circuits.

 - Partner with the FE architect and firmware lead to keep model, RTL, and firmware views coherent.

Required

 - 8+ years in architecture/performance modelling or virtual-platform development for complex SoCs.

 - Strong SystemC and TLM-2.0; solid modern C++.

 - Built or led a virtual platform / ESL model that real firmware ran on for pre-silicon bring-up.

 - Integrating a third-party ISS (e.g., Arm or RISC-V) into a SystemC/TLM platform; modelling buses, memory maps, and peripherals.

 - Architecture/performance modelling judgment — knowing what to abstract versus model faithfully.

 - Comfortable spanning hardware micro-architecture and embedded software.

Preferred

Senior Director, Manufacturing Operations

As Senior Director, Manufacturing Operations, you will own and drive the strategy and execution of our manufacturing organization. In this pivotal role, you will be responsible for capital management, capital planning, capacity modeling, and logistics, ensuring that Lumilens’ production operations enable the company’s rapid growth and operational excellence. Reporting to executive leadership, you will partner cross-functionally with engineering, supply chain, and external suppliers to seamlessly scale manufacturing capacity.

 

Key Responsibilities

 - Lead end-to-end manufacturing capital management and strategic capital planning for high-growth AI photonics products.

 - Develop and optimize capacity models and long-term manufacturing roadmaps to align with business objectives and product launch schedules.

 - Design and implement scalable logistics strategies, managing global supply chains and ensuring effective distribution and material handling.

 - Own manufacturing site selection, readiness, and ramp; oversee contract manufacturer (CM) and supplier relationships across geographies.

 - Drive process improvements, efficiency initiatives, and cost reduction across manufacturing operations.

 - Monitor and report on key operational metrics, including throughput, capacity utilization, yield, and quality.

 - Recruit, lead, and mentor a skilled manufacturing operations team; build an organizational culture of operational excellence and continuous improvement.

 

Required Qualifications

 - Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Engineering, Manufacturing, Operations, Supply Chain Management, or a related field.

 - 10+ years of experience in manufacturing operations, capital planning, or industrial engineering in high-tech sectors (e.g., semiconductors, photonics, advanced electronics).

 - Demonstrated experience with large-scale capital and capacity planning, factory operations, and international logistics.

 - Strong quantitative and analytical skills; expertise in operational modeling, forecasting, and supply chain optimization.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a track record of leading cross-functional and multi-site teams.

 

Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience with optical, semiconductor, or electronics products in a rapid-growth startup environment.

 - Familiarity with PLM systems, lean manufacturing, Six Sigma, and process improvement methodologies.

 - Experience managing global CMs and suppliers in Asia (Taiwan, China, Malaysia, Thailand).

 

What We Offer

 - Competitive salary and meaningful equity in a high-growth company

Global travel is required to CM and Supplier sites, especially during capacity ramp and supplier qualification events.
